body.title: Web UI: show open ticket queue
body.result: /queue page + /api/queue: open tickets grouped by status, 30s auto-refresh, nav link, 11 tests
body.description:
The web interface has no view of the open ticket queue. Akien can't see what's planned, in-progress, or blocked without running cc_queue.py from the terminal. Add a Queue tab (or section on the main dashboard) that shows the open tickets grouped by status (in_progress, sprint, pending, hold) with id, title, size, worker, and gate fields. Read-only for now — actions (close, block, requeue) are a follow-on.

**Affected files:** devices/web_server/server.py (new /api/queue route + UI section)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; log at INFO when queue route is accessed; OOP-first (route handler as method)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: GET /api/queue returns open tickets as JSON; web UI renders them in a Queue tab grouped by status; auto-refreshes every 30s. Out of scope: ticket actions from UI (close/block/claim), filtering/sorting beyond status grouping.
**Completion criteria:** (1) GET /api/queue returns all non-closed tickets; (2) web UI Queue tab displays them grouped by status; (3) auto-refresh every 30s; (4) tests: route returns correct ticket list; closed tickets excluded
